# paren-fuzz seed cases — known-mismatch regressions pinned for replay.
# Lines beginning with `#` and blank lines are ignored. Each SQL is
# replayed as an additional probe after the PRNG-generated batch.
#
# Category: inner JOIN missing ON/USING inside parens (PAREN-KB-1).
#   PG rejects with 42601 ("syntax error at or near `)`"); omni accepts
#   as JoinExpr with nil qual. Tracked in pg/parser/PAREN_KNOWN_BUGS.md
#   as PAREN-KB-1 with recommended fix location parseJoinedTable /
#   parseJoinQual. Also documented in
#   paren_oracle_from_degenerate_test.go (§2.7 "inner JOIN missing qual")
#   where the canonical probe is skipped with a pointer back to the bug
#   ID. The fuzz harness rediscovers it at depth here, confirming the
#   bug surfaces on more than the hand-written canonical case.
SELECT * FROM (T JOIN U)
SELECT * FROM (foo LEFT JOIN W)
SELECT * FROM (foo RIGHT OUTER JOIN W)

# Category (removed): trailing SELECT after paren-wrapped subquery.
#   Was previously probed here — PG rejects with 42601 at the second
#   SELECT; omni Parse() currently accepts by silently truncating to the
#   first RawStmt. That is a statement-list bug (Parse-level), not a
#   `(` dispatch bug, and counting it against the fuzz budget was scope
#   leakage. Tracked as PAREN-KB-2 in pg/parser/PAREN_KNOWN_BUGS.md with
#   recommended fix location in parser.go Parse(). Seed probes removed
#   and the generator's genReservedMisuse no longer produces this shape.
